name: Bahamas, The
excerpt: Lucayan Indians inhabited the Bahama islands when Christopher COLUMBUS first set foot in the New World in 1492. British settlement of the islands began in 1647; the islands became a colony in 1783.

quick_facts.government: parliamentary democracy under a constitutional monarchy; a Commonwealth realm
quick_facts.religions: Protestant 69.9% (includes Baptist 34.9%, Anglican 13.7%, Pentecostal 8.9% Seventh Day Adventist 4.4%, Methodist 3.6%, Church of God 1.9%, Plymouth Brethren 1.6%, other Protestant 0.9%), Roman Catholic 12%, other Christian 13% (includes Jehovah's Witness 1.1%), other 0.6%, none 1.9%, unspecified 2.6% (2010 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: African descent 90.6%, White 4.7%, mixed 2.1%, other 1.9%, unspecified 0.7% (2010 est.)
quick_facts.climate: tropical marine; moderated by warm waters of Gulf Stream
quick_facts.terrain: long, flat coral formations with some low rounded hills
quick_facts.major_exports: refined petroleum, ships, aluminum, shellfish, plastics (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: strategic location adjacent to US and Cuba; extensive island chain of which 30 are inhabited
summaries.history_summary: Lucayan Indians inhabited the Bahama islands when Christopher COLUMBUS first set foot in the New World in 1492. British settlement of the islands began in 1647; the islands became a colony in 1783. Piracy thrived in the 17th and 18th centuries because of The Bahamas' close proximity to shipping lanes. Since gaining independence from the UK in 1973, The Bahamas has prospered through tourism, international banking, and investment management, which comprise up to 85% of GDP. Because of its proximity to the US -- the nearest Bahamian landmass is only 80 km (50 mi) from Florida -- the country is a major transshipment point for illicit trafficking to the US mainland, as well as to Europe. US law enforcement agencies cooperate closely with The Bahamas; the Drug Enforcement Administration, US Coast Guard, and US Customs and Border Protection assist Bahamian authorities with maritime security and law enforcement through Operation Bahamas, Turks and Caicos, or OPBAT.
summaries.government_summary: parliamentary democracy under a constitutional monarchy; a Commonwealth realm. Independence: 10 July 1973 (from the UK)
summaries.economy_summary: high-income tourism and financial services economy; major income inequality; strong US bilateral relations; several tax relief programs; targeted investment in agriculture, energy, light manufacturing, and technology industries

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: The Bahamas already has a strong Baptist and Protestant majority — real ground to build on rather than a field starting from nothing. Pray for these churches to be marked by genuine, Scripture-centered discipleship rather than nominal church membership.
prayer_points: Pray for the Bahamas' extensive Baptist and Protestant churches to be strengthened in sound biblical doctrine.
Pray for pastors to be equipped in expository, Scripture-centered preaching.
Pray for genuine gospel conversion and discipleship across a culture where church attendance is common but depth of faith varies.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: With over a third of the population identifying as Baptist, the Bahamas has a substantial existing evangelical foundation. Ongoing needs include deepening biblical literacy and expository preaching among existing churches, and ensuring widespread church attendance reflects genuine gospel conversion.
